commit.message:
fix: add missing bare-name aliases for State schools

The stopword list strips "state" from team names, so bare names like
"North Carolina State" or "Colorado State" (without mascot) would
incorrectly collapse to a shorter form and potentially match the wrong
team. Added explicit aliases to resolve them to the canonical mascot name
before normalization occurs.

commit.message:
refactor: Tune ATR multipliers for profit maximization

- SL: ATR*0.7 → ATR*1.5 (research consensus for crypto, prevents premature stop-outs)
- Trail distance: ATR*0.5 → ATR*1.0 (holds through normal BTC 1H pullbacks ~$300-400)
- Trail fallback: trail_price*1.5% → trail_price*1.0% (tighter ATR=0 fallback)

Position size auto-compensates: wider SL → fewer contracts, max loss stays at $15.
ATR*0.7 was hitting 0.5% clamp constantly (sizing formula was broken).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: touch reorder with fade animation in Configure Engagement modal

to new position → 180ms fade-in. Double-click guard (ce-animating flag)
prevents queued moves during animation. Edge items (first up / last down)
are silent no-ops. .ce-move-btn removed from stub handler. (JS v23)

commit.message:
fix: resolve 1-job bug — external_id extraction + NULLS NOT DISTINCT conflict

Root causes of only 1 job inserting despite 80 fetched:

1. extractJobId regex /\/jobs\/view\/(\d+)/ failed for slug-format LinkedIn
   URLs like /jobs/view/react-dev-at-acme-1234567890/ — id came back null
   for most jobs.

2. With external_id=null, plain insert() was used. The DB unique constraint
   UNIQUE NULLS NOT DISTINCT (source_platform, external_id) treats all null
   pairs as identical — so only the first null-id row inserted; the rest
   failed silently into skipped++.

Fixes:
- extractJobId now matches all 8+ digit sequences in the URL (handles slug
  format), with URL path fallback so external_id is never null
- Route always uses upsert (external_id is guaranteed non-null)
- Added 22 tests covering extractJobId, HTML parsing, and scrapeJobsForPersonas

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

parameterized templates handle those correctly).

commit.message:
Fix exhibit detection matching inline refs instead of headings (v20260306-H)

Previous pattern matched "Exhibit A" anywhere in text, including inline
references like "as set forth in Exhibit A" — causing almost everything
to be attributed to an exhibit.

Fix: require exhibit/schedule/annex to appear at the START of a line
(anchored to ^/\r\n) so only actual section headings match.

Also restructured inferSectionFromDoc to search original docText (not
normalized) so newlines are preserved for line-anchored pattern matching.
Falls back to whitespace-normalized search if verbatim match fails.

stays in the UI so admin can act immediately after re-authenticating
through the gate without having to hunt for the button.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix tracker losing dancers during camera movement

Three improvements to SimpleTracker:
1. Keep unmatched tracks alive for 30 frames (max_missing) instead of
   immediately discarding them, so brief occlusions don't create new IDs
2. Positional fallback: when IoU and centroid both fail but the same
   number of people are detected, match by left-to-right spatial order
   (handles camera pans where all bboxes shift simultaneously)
3. Only create new track IDs for truly unmatched detections after all
   fallback strategies are exhausted

Fix multi-face selection: flatten cachedFaces to match Python's expected format

but the server was passing back the raw response which nests these
under faceBox. This caused KeyError: 'x' in photo_analyzer.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase 7: D3D11 rendering backend — device init, execute buffer parser, texture management

Add a full Direct3D 11 rendering backend that translates the game's D3D5
execute buffer submissions into modern D3D11 draw calls:

- D3D11 device/swapchain (feature level 11.0, 640x480, FLIP_DISCARD)
- HLSL shaders: vs_tlvertex (screen-to-NDC), ps_textured, ps_solid
- Execute buffer parser: D3DOP_TRIANGLE, D3DOP_STATERENDER, D3DOP_EXIT
- Texture handle table with lazy GPU upload (RGB565 → BGRA8)
- 2D surface blit for menus/HUD (Lock/Unlock → staging texture)
- Blend states (opaque, alpha, additive), depth states, rasterizer states
- IDirect3DTexture mock with GetHandle/Load for texture handle tracking
- COM method wiring: BeginScene/EndScene/Execute/Flip/SetViewport
- Linked d3d11.lib, dxgi.lib, dxguid.lib, d3dcompiler.lib

Window shows stable dark blue clear at 60fps. No rendering commands issued
yet because sub_005397D0 (game tick) remains stubbed.

commit.message:
fix: use source_key verification for accurate per-key submission audit

The submitter: modifier in /intelligence/search does not filter by
individual API key — it returns group-wide or global results. This
rewrite uses /files/{hash}/submissions to check each file's source_key,
which is a unique per-API-key fingerprint stamped on every submission.

Workflow:
1. Resolve user/group info via /users and /groups endpoints
2. Use submitter:me + fs: date range to get group-scoped candidates
3. Verify each file's submissions for the caller's source_key
4. Export only confirmed matches to CSV

Adds --source-key flag to skip auto-detection on subsequent runs.

Retrieved from Pi — allows resetting admin password by placing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: parse `module` as expression when followed by `.`, process .js input files

- Parser: `module.exports = ...` was misparse as module declaration;
  now checks if `module` is followed by `.`, `=`, etc. and treats it
  as an expression statement
- TypeScriptCompiler: process .js/.jsx input files through the pipeline
  instead of skipping them (TypeScript re-emits JS files with allowJs)
- Emitter: add space between `function` and `(` in anonymous function
  declarations (`export default function ()` not `function()`)

commit.message:
Disable TouchManager fidget-toy system while editor is active

TouchManager was capturing all touchstart/touchmove/touchend events on
the canvas with preventDefault(), completely blocking the editor's
pointer events on mobile. Added an `enabled` flag that early-returns
from all handlers when false. Engine disables it on editor enter and
re-enables on exit via new onEnter callback.

commit.message:
F1: ICESCR enforcement research post + interim caveat on /gap and /evidence

Blog post: 'Has the ICESCR Actually Worked? What the Enforcement Record Shows'
- Documents enforcement gap on Article 6 (right to work) honestly
- No documented case of ICESCR Article 6 enforced against technology-driven
  displacement in any ratifying nation's courts
- Adjacent enforcement (Italy DPA/Bologna, EU Platform Work Directive) shows
  what framework-enabled enforcement looks like
- CESCR GC 25 (2020) anchors Article 15 to AI/technology displacement nexus
- Precautionary argument: enforcement develops within frameworks, not outside
- Sources: OHCHR, ESCR-Net, ETUI, Cambridge Business and Human Rights Journal

/gap and /evidence: Interim enforcement caveat linking to the research post.
Honest about the gap while preserving the precautionary argument.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Rebuild liquidity reversion backtest with realistic execution model

Separate modular architecture: ImpactDetector (signal), FillSimulator
(latency + VWAP fills), PositionSizer (notional sizing + volume caps),
PositionManager (risk limits + exits). All params in BacktestConfig.

Key fixes for backtest realism:
- Latency simulation: orders fill at VWAP of next N trades after delay
- Notional position sizing: fixed 300 shares, not $300/price leverage
- Entry price band filter [0.15, 0.85] blocks cheap token lottery
- Resolution proximity filter blocks entries <4h from close
- Forced exit <1h from resolution
- Per-market concentration limits (3 positions, $2K notional)
- Volume cap: positions capped at 2% of trailing 1h volume
- Process all 8M trades (no 5x sampling artifact)
- Transaction cost estimate (1% round-trip)
- Daily Sharpe from equity curve, not per-trade

Output: trade_log.csv, equity_curve.csv, structured metrics with
sanity checks (Sharpe, fill rate, concentration, hold time).

commit.message:
feat: implement SchedulingService with deterministic window computation

Add the core scheduling engine for Sprint 3. SchedulingService aggregates
team member availability, subtracts busy blocks (unavailable windows and
existing events), and produces ranked time windows using a sweep-line
algorithm. Includes cross-team window intersection for negotiation.

New files:
- SchedulingService.kt — findAvailableWindows() + intersectWindows()
- SchedulingController.kt — POST /teams/:teamId/suggest-windows
- SchedulingDtos.kt — SuggestWindowsRequest, TimeWindowDto
- SchedulingServiceTest.kt — 21 unit tests (113/113 total passing)
- Bruno integration tests for suggest-windows endpoint (3 tests)

Modified:
- EventRepository.kt — added findOverlappingEvents() query
- IMPLEMENTATION_TRACKING.md — Sprint 3 progress (7/16 tasks complete)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Redesign district page election history from table to card-based timeline

Replace table-based election history with div-based vertical timeline matching
candidate.html's card pattern. All candidates now shown inline per election card
(winners highlighted, sorted first) instead of winner promoted to main row with
others hidden. Shared rendering helpers eliminate ~230 lines of duplication
between buildHistoryTable and buildUnifiedHistoryTable.

fix: gyroscope cleanup bug + move wave bars to avatar mouth

Gyroscope fix:
- Added _cleanupGyro() that resets _gyroActive, cancels RAF,
  removes deviceorientation/mousemove listeners, and clears state
- Stored handler references (_gyroOrientHandler, _gyroMouseHandler)
  so they can be properly removed on cleanup
- Called _cleanupGyro() from cleanup() so re-opening VA works

Wave bars moved to mouth:
- Moved .va-wave HTML from outside .va-orb to inside it
- Repositioned to top:46% (mouth area) instead of bottom:-44px
- Scaled down bars for mouth-sized proportions (max 22px vs 34px)
- JS audio viz max height reduced from 38 to 24
- Changed CSS selectors from ~ (sibling) to descendant
- Hidden mouth-overlay during speaking (wave bars replace it)
